title: DNA Ethanol Precipitation (SOP009.v1.1)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"cd0gb","text":"Document Summary: This document, SOP002 - DNA Ethanol Precipitation, describes a method to concentrate or dry DNA using ethanol to reduce the solubility of dissolved DNA causing it to precipitate out of solution.  Alternatively, a vacuum concentrator can be used if one is available. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ac05","text":"Quick Overview:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3rkjs","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bs0jo","text":"Step 1 – Precipitate DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2fes5","text":"Step 2 – Pellet DNA and dry","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1o7nu","text":"Step 3 – Resuspend DNA","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5u12r","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"74mtu","text":"v1.1 revision notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cff8f","text":"Updated SOP to standard formatting","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":34}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cpm8n","text":"Updated document description","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":28}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"18asm","text":"For hazard information and safety warnings, please refer to the SDS (Safety Data Sheet).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Add 1/10 volume of 3 M Na-Acetate pH 5.2 , and 2 to 2.5 volumes of ice-cold 100% ethanol to the DNA sample.
Mix, and store at -20 °C for at least 01:00:00 to precipitate the DNA. Typically, DNA will be left at -20 °C Overnight before proceeding to part 2.
Recover the precipitated DNA by centrifugation at full speed in a microcentrifuge for 00:15:00 - 00:20:00 to form a pellet.
Pour off the ethanol and wash the pellet twice with Room temperature 70 % (v/v), ethanol .
Allow the DNA pellet to air-dry.
Re-suspend the DNA in a suitable volume of sterile TE buffer or nuclease-free water.
Store at -20 °C and avoid any unnecessary freeze-thaw cycles.
creator: Rory Kruithoff
